chapter: Chapter: Keeping Time with the Skies

questions.question: What causes day and night?
How long does Earth take to complete one rotation?
What is a year based on?
questions.answer: The revolution of the Earth around the Sun.

questions.question: How many days are there in a year?
What celestial body reflects sunlight and shines at night?
What causes the phases of the Moon?
questions.answer: The Moon’s movement around the Earth.

questions.question: About how many days does the Moon take to complete one cycle of phases?
What is a constellation?
questions.answer: A group of stars forming a pattern in the sky.

questions.question: Name one famous constellation.
Which star helps find the north direction?
What instrument did ancient people use to tell time using sunlight?
What does a sundial measure?
What movement of Earth causes different seasons?
questions.answer: The revolution of the Earth around the Sun.

questions.question: Why do shadows change during the day?
questions.answer: Because the Earth rotates and the Sun’s position appears to change.

questions.question: What calendar is based on the movement of the Moon?
What calendar is based on the Earth’s revolution around the Sun?
Why were stars important to ancient travelers?
What science studies stars and planets?
Why do stars appear to move across the sky?
What is the bright star that appears almost fixed in the sky?
What helped early people measure months?
What causes the apparent movement of the Sun across the sky?
Why did early farmers observe the sky?
questions.answer: To understand seasons and plan farming activities.

questions.question: What helps astronomers identify stars easily?
Why is studying the sky important for measuring time?
questions.answer: Because the movements of celestial bodies follow regular patterns.
